What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back:
↑ renin, ↑ angiotensin I, ↑ angiotensin II, ↑ aldosterone, ↓ K⁺

ACE inhibitors block the conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II, resulting in ↑ renin, ↑ angiotensin I, ↓ angiotensin II, and ↓ aldosterone. This mechanism underlies their antihypertensive effects and expected side effects like hyperkalemia. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System Topic

Front:
A 62-year-old man is started on lisinopril for newly diagnosed hypertension.

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back: ARBs such as valsartan increase renin, angiotensin I, and angiotensin II while decreasing aldosterone due to receptor blockade. Unlike ACE inhibitors, ARBs do not inhibit synthesis of angiotensin II—they block its action at the AT₁ receptor. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System Topic

Front:
A 67-year-old man with long-standing hypertension and recent diagnosis of heart failure with reduced ejection fraction is started on spironolactone as part of guideline-directed medical therapy.

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back: Spironolactone blocks aldosterone receptors, leading to increased renin, angiotensin I, angiotensin II, and aldosterone, along with increased potassium. It is anti-androgenic and can cause gynecomastia, especially compared to eplerenone. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System Topic

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back: Enalapril decreases afterload by vasodilating systemic arterioles and increases efferent arteriolar diameter in the kidney, while leaving afferent arteriolar tone unchanged. These effects are mediated by blocking angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor. Physiolo Pharmacology (General Principles) (Mechanisms gy of action) Subject System

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back: In ACTH-independent Cushing syndrome due to adrenal overproduction of cortisol, ACTH is already suppressed and will not change with dexamethasone administration. Cortisol will also remain elevated because the adrenal gland produces it autonomously. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System Topic

Front:
A 30-year-old woman with a known parathyroid adenoma is started on a new medication as part of her treatment.

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back:
↓ Serum Ca²⁺, ↑ Serum PO₄³⁻, ↓ Urinary Ca²⁺, ↓ PTH

Cinacalcet increases the sensitivity of calcium-sensing receptors in the parathyroid glands, leading to suppressed PTH secretion. The downstream effects include decreased serum calcium, increased serum phosphate, and decreased urinary calcium. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System Topic

Front:
A 59-year-old man comes to the pulmonary clinic due to progressive shortness of breath and bilateral leg swelling over the past several months.

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back:
Blocks endothelin-1 receptors

Cor pulmonale is right-sided heart failure caused by chronic pulmonary disease, characterized by normal PCWP, loud P2, and tricuspid regurgitation. The pathophysiology involves pulmonary hypertension driven by hypoxia, with increased endothelin-1 and decreased nitric oxide synthase. Bosentan is a high-yield treatment that blocks endothelin-1 receptors, reducing pulmonary vascular resistance and improving right heart function. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System Topic

Front:
A 59-year-old man comes to the clinic due to persistent fatigue and occasional cramping in his left calf while walking long distances.

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back:
Inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase

Statins inhibit HMG-CoA reductase, reducing hepatic cholesterol synthesis. This leads to upregulation of LDL receptors, increasing plasma LDL clearance. Statins are first-line therapy for ASCVD prevention due to robust mortality benefit. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System Topic

Front:
A 22-year-old man comes to the clinic for evaluation of infertility.

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back:
↓ LH, ↓ intra-testicular testosterone

Exogenous androgen use suppresses LH through negative feedback, leading to decreased intra-testicular testosterone despite high serum testosterone levels. This hormonal profile explains the testicular atrophy and infertility seen in anabolic steroid users. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System Topic

Front:
A 27-year-old woman presents with concerns about persistent nipple discharge and irregular menstrual periods.

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back:
Agonism at dopamine D2 receptors in the anterior pituitary

Dopamine D2 receptor agonists are the mainstay of treatment for prolactinomas, as they directly inhibit prolactin secretion from lactotrophs in the anterior pituitary. Other receptor pathways—including D1, 5-HT, α1, and β2—are not involved in this regulatory mechanism. Physiol Pharmacology (General Principles) No ogy (Mechanisms of action) ne Subject System

↓ Cardiac output, ↑ heart rate, ↓ venous return

Binds estrogen receptors in the hypothalamus and blocks negative feedback signaling

Clomiphene citrate induces ovulation by blocking estrogen receptors in the hypothalamus, which removes negative feedback inhibition and results in increased GnRH, FSH, and LH release — thereby stimulating follicular maturation and ovulation. A 61-year-old man is brought to emergency by ambulance for sudden loss of consciousness while engaged in sexual activity with his wife.

Monoamine oxidase inhibitors block the presynaptic breakdown of monoamines, leading to increased vesicular storage and synaptic release. Leukotrienes are potent bronchoconstrictors derived from arachidonic acid via the lipoxygenase pathway and play a central role in asthma. A 67-year-old postmenopausal woman comes to the clinic for follow-up of bone density testing.

Inducing osteoclast apoptosis

Osteoporosis is diagnosed by low bone density on DEXA and typically presents with normal laboratory values. Postmenopausal estrogen deficiency increases osteoclast activity and bone resorption. He denies shortness of breath, wheezing, or chest pain. His past medical history includes type 2 diabetes mellitus, hyperlipidemia, and stage 1 chronic kidney disease. He takes metformin, atorvastatin, and aspirin. His blood pressure today is 138/84 mm Hg, and physical examination is unremarkable. Vasopressin (antidiuretic hormone) increases water reabsorption in the collecting ducts, leading to decreased serum sodium and serum osmolality due to dilution. Intentional ingestion of thyroid hormone supplements

Exogenous thyroid hormone ingestion causes thyrotoxicosis with suppressed TSH, elevated T3/T4, low radioactive iodine uptake, a small atrophic thyroid gland, and biopsy showing follicular atrophy and scant colloid. Front:
A 74-year-old woman presents to the emergency department with severe skin discomfort and deep discoloration over her lower abdomen and upper thighs.

What is the best answer/mechanism/drug?
Back:
Protein C deficiency

Warfarin-induced skin necrosis results from early depletion of protein C, a vitamin K–dependent anticoagulant with a short half-life, creating a transient hypercoagulable state. This leads to microvascular thrombosis and painful skin necrosis, typically within the first few days of therapy. Warfarin remains a first-line treatment for atrial fibrillation, but it must be initiated cautiously with appropriate bridging in high-risk patients.
